subject: Mathematics
chapter: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables

key_points: A pair of linear equations can be solved graphically or algebraically
Graphical solution represents the point of intersection of two lines
Algebraic methods include substitution and elimination
A system is consistent if it has at least one solution
Nature of solutions depends on ratios of coefficients
examples: x + y = 5 and x − y = 1 intersect at one point
formulas: If a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂ → no solution
If a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂ → infinite solutions
exam_tips: Check coefficient ratios before solving fully
Graphical method is useful for visual understanding
Identify nature of solution before solving
